chapters.title: Effects of Electric Current
chapters.description: Heating effect, power, resistance, safety devices and domestic electric circuits.

test.title: Effects of Electric Current - Chapter Test

questions.text: Which effect of electric current is used in an electric heater?
questions.explanation: An electric heater converts electrical energy into heat due to the heating effect of current.

questions.text: The SI unit of electric power is:
questions.explanation: Electric power is the rate of doing electrical work. Its SI unit is watt.

questions.text: Which relation gives the heat produced in a resistor?
questions.explanation: Joule's law of heating is H = I^2Rt.

questions.text: A fuse wire is connected in a circuit to:
questions.explanation: A fuse melts and breaks the circuit when current exceeds the safe limit.

questions.text: For a given voltage, current is inversely proportional to:
questions.explanation: By Ohm's law, I = V/R. At fixed voltage, current decreases when resistance increases.

questions.text: Which material is commonly used as the heating element in appliances?
questions.explanation: Nichrome has high resistance and high melting point, so it is suitable for heating elements.

questions.text: One unit of electrical energy used in household billing is:
questions.explanation: Electricity consumption is commonly measured in kilowatt hour.

questions.text: Earthing of appliances mainly helps to:
questions.explanation: Earthing provides a low resistance path for leakage current and protects users from electric shock.

chapters.title: Magnetic Effect of Electric Current
chapters.description: Magnetic field around current carrying conductors, solenoids, motors and electromagnets.

test.title: Magnetic Effect of Electric Current - Chapter Test

questions.text: A current carrying conductor produces:
questions.explanation: An electric current produces a magnetic field around the conductor.

questions.text: The magnetic field lines around a straight current carrying conductor are:
questions.explanation: Around a straight conductor, magnetic field lines form concentric circles.

questions.text: A solenoid behaves like:
questions.explanation: A current carrying solenoid has north and south poles like a bar magnet.

questions.text: The device that converts electrical energy into mechanical energy is:
questions.explanation: An electric motor converts electrical energy into mechanical motion.

questions.text: The strength of an electromagnet can be increased by:
questions.explanation: More turns and larger current increase the magnetic field of an electromagnet.

questions.text: The direction of force on a current carrying conductor in a magnetic field is found using:
questions.explanation: Fleming's left hand rule gives the direction of force in a motor effect.

questions.text: The magnetic field inside a long solenoid is nearly:
questions.explanation: Inside a long solenoid, the magnetic field is strong and nearly uniform.

questions.text: A split ring commutator in a DC motor helps to:
questions.explanation: The split ring reverses current in the coil every half turn, keeping rotation in the same direction.

chapters.title: Electromagnetic Induction
chapters.description: Induced emf, generators, alternating current, transformers and power transmission.

questions.text: Electromagnetic induction is the production of induced current due to:
questions.explanation: Induced emf is produced when magnetic flux linked with a circuit changes.

questions.text: The direction of induced current is given by:
questions.explanation: Lenz's law states that induced current opposes the change that produces it.

questions.text: An electric generator converts:
questions.explanation: A generator uses electromagnetic induction to convert mechanical work into electrical energy.

questions.text: The current supplied by most power stations is:
questions.explanation: Power stations commonly generate alternating current for transmission.

questions.text: A transformer works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: A transformer transfers energy between coils by mutual induction.

questions.text: A step-up transformer increases:
questions.explanation: A step-up transformer has more turns in the secondary coil and increases voltage.

questions.text: Power loss in transmission lines can be reduced by transmitting at:
questions.explanation: For the same power, high voltage reduces current and therefore reduces I^2R loss.

questions.text: The frequency of AC in Indian domestic supply is:
questions.explanation: Indian domestic AC supply has a frequency of 50 Hz.

chapters.title: Reflection of Light
chapters.description: Reflection laws, spherical mirrors, image formation, mirror formula and magnification.

questions.text: The angle of incidence is equal to:
questions.explanation: According to the law of reflection, angle of incidence equals angle of reflection.

questions.text: The image formed by a plane mirror is:
questions.explanation: A plane mirror forms a virtual, erect image of the same size as the object.

questions.text: A concave mirror can form:
questions.explanation: A concave mirror forms real images for many object positions and a virtual image when the object is between pole and focus.

questions.text: The focus of a convex mirror is:
questions.explanation: A convex mirror has a virtual focus behind the mirror.

questions.text: The relation between focal length and radius of curvature of a spherical mirror is:
questions.explanation: For a spherical mirror, focal length is half the radius of curvature.

questions.text: Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors because they:
options: Give a wider field of view
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors form diminished erect images and provide a wider field of view.

questions.text: The mirror formula is:
questions.explanation: The mirror formula relates focal length, object distance and image distance: 1/f = 1/u + 1/v.

questions.text: A real image is formed when reflected rays:
options: Appear to diverge from behind the mirror
questions.explanation: A real image is formed by actual convergence of reflected rays.

chapters.title: Refraction of Light
chapters.description: Refraction, refractive index, lenses, image formation, lens formula and power.

questions.text: Refraction is the bending of light when it:
options: Passes from one medium to another
questions.explanation: Light bends at the boundary between two media because its speed changes.

questions.text: When light travels from air to glass, it bends:
questions.explanation: Glass is optically denser than air, so light bends towards the normal.

questions.text: A convex lens is also called a:
questions.explanation: A convex lens converges parallel rays to its focus.

questions.text: A concave lens usually forms an image that is:
questions.explanation: A concave lens diverges light and forms a virtual, erect, diminished image for a real object.

questions.text: Power of a lens is measured in:
questions.explanation: Lens power is measured in dioptre.

questions.text: The power of a lens with focal length 0.5 m is:
questions.explanation: Power P = 1/f in metres = 1/0.5 = 2 D.

questions.text: The refractive index of a medium equals:
options: Speed of light in medium / speed in vacuum
Speed of light in vacuum / speed in medium
questions.explanation: Absolute refractive index n = c/v.

questions.text: Total internal reflection occurs when light travels from:
options: Rarer to denser medium at any angle
Denser to rarer medium beyond critical angle
questions.explanation: Total internal reflection occurs from denser to rarer medium when incidence angle exceeds the critical angle.

chapters.title: Vision and the World of Colours
chapters.title_ml: Vision and the World of Colours
chapters.description: Human eye, defects of vision, dispersion, scattering and colour phenomena.

test.title: Vision and the World of Colours - Chapter Test

questions.text: The part of the eye that acts like a lens is:
questions.explanation: The eye lens focuses light on the retina.

questions.text: The image formed on the retina is:
questions.explanation: The eye lens forms a real and inverted image on the retina.

questions.text: Myopia is corrected using:
questions.explanation: Myopia or short-sightedness is corrected using a concave lens.

questions.text: Hypermetropia is corrected using:
questions.explanation: Hypermetropia or long-sightedness is corrected using a convex lens.

questions.text: Splitting of white light into its component colours is called:
questions.explanation: Dispersion separates white light into different colours.

questions.text: The blue colour of the sky is mainly due to:
questions.explanation: Shorter wavelength blue light is scattered more by the atmosphere.

questions.text: The band of colours obtained from white light is called:
questions.explanation: A spectrum is the ordered band of colours formed by dispersion.

questions.text: The screen of the eye on which image is formed is the:
questions.explanation: The retina is the light-sensitive screen of the eye.

chapters.title: Energy Management
chapters.description: Energy sources, conservation, efficiency, sustainable use and environmental impact.

questions.text: Which of the following is a renewable source of energy?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable because it is naturally replenished.

questions.text: The main aim of energy management is to:
questions.explanation: Energy management focuses on efficient and responsible use of energy.

questions.text: LED lamps are preferred because they:
questions.explanation: LED lamps are energy efficient and reduce electricity consumption.

questions.text: Fossil fuels cause environmental concern mainly because they:
questions.explanation: Burning fossil fuels releases pollutants and greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide.

questions.text: The star rating on electrical appliances indicates:
questions.explanation: A higher star rating generally means better energy efficiency.

questions.text: Which action helps conserve electrical energy at home?
questions.explanation: Efficient appliances reduce energy consumption for the same useful output.

questions.text: Hydroelectric power plants mainly convert:
options: Energy of flowing water to electrical energy
questions.explanation: Hydroelectric plants use the mechanical energy of flowing water to generate electricity.

questions.text: Sustainable energy use means:
options: Using resources without considering the future
Meeting present needs without harming future needs
questions.explanation: Sustainable use balances present energy needs with environmental protection and future availability.
